content: Now we need to talk about the /agentic-engineering Layer again. Now that we have our session structure set up and the agents to maintain the documentation for the agents, we need to talk about the skill for this specific project. I have created a /library Skill in my library for creating web frontends that I have used in another project. I would like to bring that in as a base. That skill, as it sits in the library, has templates in there that would be at this stage localized, installed, and kind of set up. Take a look at this and see if you understand that

Yes rename on copy It was my vision to run the web scaffold just once anyway so you're reading that correctly I would copy them into the repo so they can be improved and further localized as the project evolves Yes we should plan on being able to either use the WOFF2 files and for other typography, which we'll talk about as we go. Look to the macOS typography skill We won't localize it but inside there should be details about the Adobe Creative Cloud subscription because we'll be able to utilize some of those licensed fonts on the web that way too And yes I think your handling of the pie harness at this point is good Go ahead and draft the plan please. You're going to want to ensure that when we bring in the MCPs, we make sure that those are all properly configured and that those sub-agents or top-level agents have total mastery of the surface that is made availablle Through the Claude and Chrome built-in MCP or the Playwright CLI, which can be just as efficient if not more
We will go ahead and plan on configuring Playwright inside of this project so that we can use the Playwright CLI
Yeah that's over, Kel. Go ahead and continue

content: The scaffolding and architecture of the project is already starting to drift beyond what I would like to see. /engineering-management Should be able to give us some better perspective on how we can have a cleaner structure. Right now we've got scripts and tools that are used by the agentic layer sitting side-by-side with actual content. This is no good. We have standards. Let's get this up to standard

Can we keep the actual site in a folder for the site instead of having the files maintained in the root? It will be a full website over time not just one single page We're going to be sticking lean; however, in that we'll make totally portable pages that we should be able to just push To a properly configured repo Hopefully I want to avoid site builders and all that other fucking garbage and/or all that CI/CD shit on the other end. I know that GitHub Pages is going to have its own configuration and I know that we need a dedicated skill set with agents and predefined processes for publishing that is based on web research. That is going to be part of my next phase of building the agentic layer I don't know what the py-agents.yml file is, if it needs to be in the root or if it needs to be somewhere else. That would be a convention that is dictated by the py-agent documentation not by me necessarily. If it's got to be there, it's got to be there, kinda like the claude.md
Can you dispatch sub-agents to do it? Doing it that way would be way more token efficient and reliable. One agent per task. Verify the code, verify against the spec
